heading: Education and risk management assistance
label: 1524 Education and risk management assistance

L. 107–171“(1) .—The Secretary shall provide cost share assistance to producers, in a manner determined by the Secretary, in not less than 10, nor more than 15, States in which participation in the Federal crop insurance program is historically low, as determined by the Secretary.Authority“(2) .—A producer may use cost share assistance provided under this subsection to—Uses“(A) construct or improve—“(i) watershed management structures; or“(ii) irrigation structures;“(B) plant trees to form windbreaks or to improve water quality;“(C) mitigate financial risk through production diversification or resource conservation practices, including—“(i) soil erosion control;“(ii) integrated pest management; or“(iii) transition to organic farming;“(D) enter into futures, hedging, or options contracts in a manner designed to help reduce production, price, or revenue risk;“(E) enter into agricultural trade options as a hedging transaction to reduce production, price, or revenue risk; or“(F) conduct any other activity related to the activities described in subparagraphs (A) through (E), as determined by the Secretary.“(2) .—The total amount of payments made to a person (as defined in ) under this subsection for any year may not exceed $50,000.Payment limitationsection 1308(5) of this title“(3)Commodity credit corporation.—“(A) .—The Secretary shall carry out this subsection through the Commodity Credit Corporation.In general“(B) .—The Commodity Credit Corporation shall make available to carry out this subsection $10,000,000 for fiscal year 2001 and each subsequent fiscal year.”Funding

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to the amounts made available under paragraph (4), the Secretary, acting through the National Institute of Food and Agriculture, shall carry out the program established under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Authority
children.label: A Authority
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the National Institute of Food and Agriculture, shall establish a program under which competitive grants are made to qualified public and private entities (including land grant colleges, cooperative extension services, and colleges or universities), as determined by the Secretary, for the purpose of educating agricultural producers and providing technical assistance to agricultural producers on a full range of farm viability and risk management activities, including futures, options, agricultural trade options, crop insurance, business planning, enterprise analysis, transfer and succession planning, management coaching, market assessment, cash flow analysis, cash forward contracting, debt reduction, production diversification, farm resources risk reduction, farm financial benchmarking, conservation activities, and other risk management strategies.

children.heading: Basis for grants
children.label: B Basis for grants
children.text: A grant under this paragraph shall be awarded on the basis of merit and shall be subject to peer or merit review.

children.heading: Obligation period
children.label: C Obligation period
children.text: Funds for a grant under this paragraph shall be available to the Secretary for obligation for a 2-year period.

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: D Administrative costs
children.text: The Secretary may use not more than 4 percent of the funds made available for grants under this paragraph for administrative costs incurred by the Secretary in carrying out this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: beginning farmers or ranchers;

children.label: B
children.text: legal immigrant farmers or ranchers that are attempting to become established producers in the United States;

children.label: C
children.text: socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers;

children.label: i
children.text: are preparing to retire;

children.label: ii
children.text: are using transition strategies to help new farmers or ranchers get started;1So in original. Probably should be followed by “and”.1

children.label: iii
children.text: are converting production and marketing systems to pursue new markets; and2So in original. The word “and” probably should not appear.2

children.label: E
children.text: producers that are underserved by the Federal crop insurance program established under this subchapter, as determined by the Corporation; and

children.label: F
children.text: veteran farmers or ranchers.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 4 Funding
children.text: From the insurance fund established under , there is transferred for the partnerships for risk management education program established under paragraph (2), $10,000,000 for fiscal year 2019 and each subsequent fiscal year, of which not less than $5,000,000 shall be used to carry out paragraph (3)(E).section 1516(c) of this title

children.heading: Authority
children.label: 1 Authority
children.text: The Secretary shall provide financial assistance to producers in the States of Connecticut, Delaware, Hawaii, Maryland, Massachusetts, Maine, Nevada, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Utah, Vermont, West Virginia, and Wyoming.

children.label: i
children.text: watershed management structures; or

children.label: ii
children.text: irrigation structures;

children.label: B
children.text: plant trees to form windbreaks or to improve water quality;

children.label: i
children.text: soil erosion control;

children.label: ii
children.text: integrated pest management;

children.label: iii
children.text: organic farming; or

children.label: iv
children.text: to develop and implement a plan to create marketing opportunities for the producer, including through value-added processing;

children.label: D
children.text: enter into futures, hedging, or options contracts in a manner designed to help reduce production, price, or revenue risk;

children.label: E
children.text: enter into agricultural trade options as a hedging transaction to reduce production, price, or revenue risk; or

children.label: F
children.text: conduct any other activity relating to an activity described in subparagraphs (A) through (E), as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Payment limitation
children.label: 3 Payment limitation
children.text: The total amount of payments made to a person (as defined in section 1308(5) of this title) (before the amendment made by section 1603(a) of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008) under this subsection for any year may not exceed $50,000.33See References in Text note below.3

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out this subsection through the Commodity Credit Corporation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the Commodity Credit Corporation shall make available to carry out this subsection not less than $10,000,000 for each fiscal year.

children.heading: Exception for certain fiscal years
children.label: ii Exception for certain fiscal years
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2008 through 2014, the Commodity Credit Corporation shall make available to carry out this subsection $15,000,000.

children.label: i
children.text: 50 percent to carry out subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of paragraph (2) through the Natural Resources Conservation Service;

children.label: ii
children.text: 10 percent to provide organic certification cost share assistance through the Agricultural Marketing Service; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 40 percent to conduct activities to carry out subparagraph (F) of paragraph (2) through the Risk Management Agency.
